entries.name: The Toshi Ranbo front — the Lion–Crane war
entries.note: PRIMARY. The Lion and Crane — ancestral enemies — are at open war over the contested town of Toshi Ranbo (Akodo Arasou slain by Doji Hotaru's arrow, 1123). Akihiko's Legion is deployed to enforce an Imperial edict between two armies that hate each other. (facts.py relation lion crane)

entries.name: Keep my soldiers alive — ninjō vs the orders that spend them
entries.note: The engine of the drama. His cohort carries a FALCON (Toritaka) contingent — clansmen serving the Legion under their famous-blooded son; his ninjō is to march them home to the Valley of the Spirits ALIVE. Every order that spends lives to win the Emperor's war pokes it. The cruelty: his giri (honor the Falcon) is paid in the same Falcon lives his ninjō would save. Trigger to watch: Softheartedness (Fire) — he seizes up about KILLING, +3 strife when he takes a life. (Also Fear of Mediocrity (Fire): +3 strife when he must improvise/adapt to the unexpected on a Fire check.)

entries.name: The black-sheep blood & uncle Seppun Tadanobu's leash
entries.note: His known Imperial blood comes from the scandalous branch — grandfather left the Seppun to marry a Toritaka; uncle Tadanobu redeemed it and sponsors Akihiko. The favor is a leash: what does the uncle expect in return, and who resents the upjumped black-sheep nephew?

entries.name: Officers who know whose nephew he is — expectation & resentment
entries.note: Akihiko serves under officers aware of his Seppun patron and his minor-clan origins. ELEVATION here is rolled, not narrated (Status-priced Fate Questions); trust and favor arrive with strings.

entries.name: The spirit-dark of the battlefield — what the Falcon sees that the war hides
entries.note: Quiet thread. A battlefield is a feast for the dead; Akihiko's Falcon spirit-sight (Sixth Sense) may find yūrei, gaki, or worse moving under cover of the mundane war. Latent — let the dice surface it.

entries.name: The buried truth — known, sealed, undrawable
entries.note: ARC-2 SLOW FUSE (from the Exc-No verdict). The full proof of the Lion's poison+looting — manifest, Throne-seals from Shizue's brazier, Ume's dated ledger, the Crane+Lion depositions — exists, sealed 'at the highest level' to keep the Right and Left Hands from open court-war mid-conflict. The TRUTH is known (the Lion host that watched, Saburō, Akihiko's men, Ume, Kasumi, Sukune) but cannot become a public verdict. It is a weapon that exists and cannot be drawn. Questions for Arc 2: who keeps the key? Does the Lion owe a quiet debt to the outsider who let them cleanse it without public shame? Can the truth ever surface — and at what cost to whoever draws it? A latent lever, dormant until the dice or the player wake it.

entries.name: Reassignment — the Legion holds the brush
entries.note: ARC-2 OPENER (from the No on choosing his posting). Akihiko keeps the rank of chūi but NOT the choice of where he goes next; he is reassigned at the Legion's discretion. Read with the Exc-No verdict: the man who nearly lit a civil war between the Hands is too capable to discard, too inconvenient to leave on a sensitive front — so his effectiveness is redirected somewhere it can't destabilize anything. WHERE they send him is the first scene of Arc 2, and the brush is theirs: toward home and the Valley of the Spirits (ironically near the shut family gate)? a lonelier, harder front where inconvenient officers are buried in plain sight? a posting that is half-reward, half-exile? To be framed/rolled when Arc 2 opens.

entries.name: The matriarch's grant — Chiyo, a rōnin bride, and the family rift
entries.note: ARC-2 ROMANCE + FAMILY thread (supersedes 'The shut gate' after the marriage-authority canon fix). Tadanobu (Seppun, married OUT) holds no say over a Toritaka match — only disfavor; he refused (Fate: No). The real authority, grandmother Toritaka CHIYO (iron daimyō, head of the line), GRANTED leave (Fate: 50/50 → 12 → YES) — WITH STRINGS: (1) she'll judge the rōnin Kasumi in person (a scene to play); (2) she'd adopt Kasumi into the Falcon vassal house to restore a name — binding Kasumi to Chiyo, indebted, and winning the house a famous Matsu duelist; (3) Akihiko's primary loyalty pulled HOME to her Falcon house, away from the Seppun uncle. Net: the romance is ALIVE on a clanless bride's terms, and a quiet GRANDMOTHER-vs-UNCLE rift opens with Akihiko the prize. Live levers: the matriarch's audience with Kasumi; the adoption's price; navigating the two relatives; restoring enough of Kasumi's face that the match honors the house.

entries.name: The Scattered Corps — silent on the line
entries.note: ARC-2 DORMANT thread (Fate: Likely → 30 → NO — none surfaced at Arc-1's close). Akihiko's real friends/colleagues — the cross-clan healers and wardens bound to him by a past crisis they survived together (see roster/setting-canon). At the largest moment of his life — the conspiracy broken, the vow kept before an army — NOT ONE was there, and word has reached none of them; war keeps them scattered across half of Rokugan on their own clans' duties. The bond is real but dark; no letter waits, no familiar face turns up. A warm distant ache, latent: one may surface when the reassignment lands him somewhere new (the dice or the player can wake it). For now: accounted for, alive as far as he knows, unreachable.
